title: What is a Febrile Seizure
content: A febrile seizure is a convulsion in young children triggered by a high fever, typically occurring between 6 months and 5 years of age. Most are benign and self-limiting.

title: Glasgow Coma Scale
content: The Glasgow Coma Scale assesses level of consciousness using eye, verbal, and motor responses, scored from 3 to 15. Lower scores indicate more severe impairment.

title: What is Epilepsy
content: Epilepsy is a chronic neurological disorder characterized by recurrent unprovoked seizures caused by abnormal electrical activity in the brain.

title: Pediatric Vital Sign Ranges
content: Normal pediatric vital signs vary by age. Infants: heart rate 100-160 bpm, respiratory rate 30-60/min. Toddlers: heart rate 90-150 bpm. Values move toward adult ranges with age.

title: What is Migraine
content: Migraine is a primary headache disorder featuring recurrent moderate-to-severe headaches, often unilateral and pulsating, sometimes accompanied by aura, nausea, and photophobia.

title: Supra Pediatric Antibiotic Policy
content: Supra Pediatrics uses Amoxicillin-clavulanate as first-line treatment for acute otitis media. Decision by Dr. Anjali Rao, March 2025. Avoid in penicillin-allergic children.

title: Patient Aarav Live Vaccine Hold
content: ABSOLUTE: Hold all live vaccines for patient Aarav (immunocompromised, post-chemotherapy). MMR and varicella are contraindicated until cleared by oncology. 2 deferrals documented.

title: EEG Machine Standard
content: Supra Neurology standardized on Natus EEG systems in both labs. Decision based on a 3-year reliability review by Dr. Anjali Rao in 2024.

title: Pediatric Ward Capacity
content: Supra Pediatric Ward: 35 beds total, with 10 NICU, 8 PICU, and 17 general. Occupancy runs at 88%. Monsoon peak reaches 100%+, activating the dengue overflow protocol.

title: Neurology Budget 2026
content: FY 2026 Neurology budget: Rs 3.1 Cr. MRI and EEG 40%, Staffing 30%, Drugs 18%, Training 12%. New EMG unit approved in Q3.

title: Febrile Seizure Management
content: Febrile seizures lasting under 5 minutes need no acute medication. Supra protocol: rectal or buccal midazolam if longer than 5 minutes, mandatory paediatric review, and a parent education leaflet issued.
non_derivable_portion: Supra protocol: rectal/buccal midazolam if >5 min; mandatory paediatric review; parent education leaflet.

title: Status Epilepticus Protocol
content: Status epilepticus is treated with a benzodiazepine first-line, then levetiracetam or phenytoin. Supra uses levetiracetam as second-line and auto-alerts the neurology team at 10 minutes.
non_derivable_portion: Supra: levetiracetam as second-line; auto-alert neurology at 10 minutes.

title: Pediatric Weight-Based Dosing Safety
content: All pediatric drug doses are calculated by weight in mg/kg and double-checked by two nurses. Supra incident 2024: a tenfold dosing near-miss prompted mandatory independent verification.
non_derivable_portion: Supra incident 2024: tenfold dosing near-miss; mandatory independent verification.

title: Abrupt Antiepileptic Withdrawal
content: Do NOT stop antiepileptic drugs abruptly due to the risk of breakthrough seizures and status epilepticus. Supra past incident: readmission after self-discontinuation. Taper gradually over weeks.
non_derivable_portion: Supra past incident: readmission after self-discontinuation.

title: Pediatric Head Injury Imaging
content: Use the PECARN criteria to decide on CT in pediatric head injury, minimizing radiation exposure. Supra threshold: senior sign-off is required before any pediatric CT, and this is audited monthly.
non_derivable_portion: Supra threshold: senior sign-off before any pediatric CT; audited monthly.
